L1.area: Sandbox code execution engine and variable scope management

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where sandboxed code can overwrite methods on safe built-in prototypes. The security check intended to prevent prototype property modification is insufficient, allowing an attacker to replace built-in methods with functions whose constructor property exposes the native Function constructor. This enables construction and execution of arbitrary code outside the sandbox, resulting in remote code execution.

L3.area: Sandbox property assignment security checks and variable scope resolution logic
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where sandboxed code can overwrite own properties of safe built-in prototypes such as Map. The property assignment security check only blocks overriding inherited prototype properties but not own properties on prototype objects. An attacker can replace a prototype method with a built-in function, then use its constructor to obtain the native Function constructor, enabling arbitrary code execution outside the sandbox.

ground_truth.reason: The sandbox allows overwriting methods on safe built-in prototypes (e.g., Map.prototype.has), enabling an attacker to access the Function constructor and execute arbitrary code outside the sandbox boundary.
